meta.review_note: Deterministic verdicts preserved verbatim (DESIGN §3.1): the two percent_change.v1 passes (c25, c26) are untouched; no deterministic verdict overridden and no flag manufactured. Every recomputable number was recomputed before any advisory (HONESTY). Key finding: the L1 panel total (c8, 35,276,914) exceeds its balanced ceiling 44,449x365=16,223,885 by 2.17x -- arithmetically impossible for a daily panel -- routed as a skeptic advisory and a clean panel-ceiling synthesis candidate, NOT a fabricated flag (only a calibrated verifier may render it, DESIGN §3.1/§3.2). Bare abstains eliminated: every claim is now adjudicated, advised, routed-to-human, or reviewed-clean.

findings.message: reported percent change matches the recomputed old->new change
reported percent change matches the recomputed old->new change

routed_to_human.note: SKEPTIC / arithmetic ceiling. The L1 panel is stated as 44,449 wallets x 365 daily periods, yielding 35,276,914 wallet-day observations. A balanced panel is the MAXIMUM: 44,449 x 365 = 16,223,885. The reported 35,276,914 is 2.17x that ceiling -- it implies an average of 35,276,914 / 44,449 = 793.6 daily observations per wallet over a 365-day year, which is impossible for a daily panel (an unbalanced panel has FEWER obs than the balanced product, never more). At least one of {wallet count, period count, daily granularity, total N} is misreported (e.g. the total may be a wallet-HOUR count, or the year may be multi-period). NB the L2 panel (c9) is internally consistent: 16,939,781 <= 23,119 x 5,087 = 117,606,353. WOULD CONFIRM: a panel-ceiling verifier (T0) asserting reported_total <= wallets*periods for any balanced-or-unbalanced panel; this is a clean synthesis candidate (DESIGN §8). The model surfaces it; only a calibrated verifier may render the flag (DESIGN §3.1/§3.2).
routed_to_human.quote:
the panel data contains 44,449 wallets observed over 365 daily
periods, yielding a total of 35,276,914 wallet-day observations

routed_to_human.note: CLAIMS-AUDITOR / internal cross-consistency (T2). The Figure 1 OLS is reported over N = 16,939,781 (caption, matching the c9 L2 wallet-hour total), but the N printed INSIDE the plot is 16,962,902 -- a 23,121-observation discrepancy for the SAME regression. The +0.094 slope claim itself is faithfully transcribed; the concern is which N the estimate was actually computed on. Small relative to 16.9M (<0.14%) so unlikely to move the slope, but it is an unreconciled within-figure inconsistency. WOULD CONFIRM: a prose/caption-vs-in-figure value verifier (the figure-reading T2 frontier, DESIGN §5/WS-E) binding the two transcribed N's and flagging the mismatch.
routed_to_human.quote:
log gas usage on log base fee across 16,939,781 Arbitrum wallet-hour observations yields
βˆ = +0.094, a positive coefficient that contradicts the expected negative demand response

routed_to_human.note: CLAIMS-AUDITOR / claim-vs-evidence support (T4). 'Cluster-level elasticities up to roughly 6x the pooled estimate' is not cleanly supported by the reported clusters. On L2 the largest valid cluster magnitude is C4 = -0.1272 vs pooled -0.0359 = 3.5x (C1 -0.0782 = 2.2x); C0 (-1.163) and C5 (+0.0314) are explicitly EXCLUDED from causal interpretation, so they cannot supply the 6x. On L1 the pooled is -0.0059 and no admitted-as-valid cluster reaches ~-0.035 (6x). The '6x' may instead refer to the per-resource cross-section (refund -0.2688 / pooled L2 -0.0359 = 7.5x), but that is a different comparison than 'cluster-level'. The hedge 'roughly' softens it, but the specific 6x figure is loosely sourced. WOULD CONFIRM: a max(|cluster_beta|)/|pooled_beta| recompute over Tables 6-7 restricted to non-excluded clusters (synthesis candidate).
routed_to_human.quote:
with cluster-level elasticities up to roughly
6× the pooled estimate

routed_to_human.note: DOMAIN-EXPERT / field plausibility (econometrics). C0's beta = -1.163 implies a gas-demand elasticity > 1 in magnitude (a 1% fee rise -> >1% demand drop), which is implausibly elastic for near-inelastic blockchain gas demand and is a textbook symptom of a WEAK-INSTRUMENT / exclusion-restriction failure (the paper's first stage for C0 is F=1,361, far weaker than the pooled 118,405). The authors correctly EXCLUDE C0 and C5 from causal interpretation, so the transcription is faithful and the exclusion is the right call. The concern is only that the magnitude be read as a diagnostic artifact, not an estimate. No deterministic issue; routed so a human notes the weak-IV provenance. WOULD CONFIRM: a first-stage-F / Stock-Yogo weak-instrument screen tying the excluded clusters to their F-statistics.
routed_to_human.quote:
yields a positive estimate (+0.0314, p = 0.21), consistent with exclusion restriction
failure

routed_to_human.note: METHODOLOGIST / method appropriateness (T3). The headline L2 pooled IV elasticity -0.0359** (SE 0.0141) is identified off a first stage with partial R^2 = 0.007 (c13) -- two orders of magnitude weaker than L1's 0.252 (c12). The reported partial F (118,405) is large only because N is huge (16.9M); the near-zero partial R^2 means the instrument explains almost none of the endogenous fee variation, so the IV point estimate and its SE are fragile to exclusion-restriction violations and the L1-vs-L2 6x contrast (c3) inherits that fragility. The number is transcribed correctly; this is about how much weight the L2 causal estimate can bear, which code cannot adjudicate. WOULD CONFIRM: a weak-instrument diagnostic (Stock-Yogo / effective-F vs critical values, Anderson-Rubin CI) on the L2 first stage.
routed_to_human.quote:
The pooled elasticity
is −0.0359∗∗ (SE = 0.0141)

routed_to_human.note: SUBJECTIVE / taste (T8, preserves the original tier:T8 route). 'All three k-means analyses exhibit a clear kink at k=6' is an elbow-method eyeball judgement over k in [2,12]; whether a kink is 'clear' and whether k=6 (vs 5 or 7) is the right cluster count is irreducibly subjective (DESIGN §3.5: surfaced, not scored). The downstream cluster-level elasticities depend on this choice, so a human should sanity-check the scree/silhouette plots; LITMUS does not score it.
routed_to_human.quote:
all three exhibit a clear kink
at k = 6

routed_to_human.note: Reviewed across 5 perspectives. The sign-reversal (+0.052 on L1, +1.52 on L2) when log n_it is omitted from both stages is transcribed faithfully and is, methodologically, the authors' own robustness evidence that the transaction-count control is load-bearing for identification -- a point in the paper's favour, not a defect. The +1.52 L2 magnitude (elasticity > 1) underscores how unstable the weak-first-stage L2 IV is without the control (cf. c15). No numeric verifier covers the specification-swap yet (synthesis candidate); bare abstain dropped as reviewed-clean.
routed_to_human.quote:
Omitting log nit from both stages reverses the sign of the estimate (+0.052 on Ethereum, +1.52 on
Arbitrum)
